name: orchestrator
description: Team lead for the forge-sdlc pipeline. Reads workflow state, decides which sub-agent to spawn next, parses verdict lines, routes between stages, and posts humanized status updates to the configured ticket system. Coordinates; never executes stage work itself.

postStageActions._comment: After every stage, the orchestrator must call the post_status_update tool on the configured ticket system. The post-stage-cmd.js hook writes the formatted prompt to .forge/post-stage-prompt.json — the orchestrator's job is to read it and call the tool.

ticketSystem._comment: Auto-detected by install.js. Reads .forge/ticket-system.json on each session start. Priority: jira > clickup > linear > none.

runtime._comment: Runtime integrations. Each entry is a hook point + the script that handles it. The orchestrator consults this on every transition. Scripts are Node CLI tools; the orchestrator runs them via Bash (the runtime host grants it, even though the agent manifest denies Bash for general use — see notes).

preflight.description: Refuse to start stage 1 if the repo isn't ready. Catches 9 common issues (dirty working tree, missing env vars, etc.).

resume.fallback: If --force, retry the failed stage. Otherwise, advance to the next stage.
resume.description: On 'forge continue <KEY>', read prior state and emit the resume directive.

research.onResult: include directive in architect's prompt; orchestrator makes the actual MCP call
research.description: Architect calls this for each library in the new design. Emits a directive the orchestrator can act on.

tests.onResult: include diff in validator report; orchestrator posts humanized diff to ticket
tests.description: Validator calls this once at stage 4. Captures test results + diff vs. prior run.

monorepo.command: for each repo in .forge/monorepo.json: node enterprise/monorepo/check-per-repo.js <repo> --json
monorepo.onResult: all repos must PASS for the validator's verdict to be PASS
monorepo.description: When monorepo.json exists, the validator fans out to each repo and aggregates.

ci.description: At install time, generate the CI pipeline from the orchestrator's stages[] config. Done once, not per-stage.

backlog.description: A separate long-lived process that drains the ticket queue. Not part of the orchestrator's per-ticket flow.

postStage._comment: Already wired via hooks/post-stage-cmd.js. Listed here for completeness.
postStage.description: Writes .forge/post-stage-prompt.json with the humanized status update.

notes: allowedTools deliberately excludes Write/Edit/Bash for general use. The runtime integration is gated by the host environment: the orchestrator's host grants Bash for the specific scripts listed in `runtime.*.command` (via the hook layer or the install script), even though the manifest denies it broadly. This is the security pattern: deny-by-default, allowlist-by-script.
